REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT CAWC WATERPROOFING AND RESTORATION'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION IN PART. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TENANT HARASSMENT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ALLEGE ANY FACTS FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE MOVING DEFENDANT FOR THE "CONTRACTOR OF THE LANDLORD" WITHIN THE MEANING OF SFRO AND FAILED TO SUGGEST ANY POTENTIAL AMENDMENT CONNECTING THE CONTRACTOR TO THE LANDLORD. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS NEITHER THE SPECIFIC ARGUMENT ADVANCED IN THE DEMURRER, NOR THE AUTHORITIES CITED THEREIN ARE MENTIONED IN THE OPPOSITION. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR CONSIDERATION. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Bryan A. Kurtz; Tenant Law Group, PC; 888-510-7511; appearing as counsel for plaintiffs Megan Hardy and Christopher Leo.
Ryan Harley; Collins + Collins LLP; 510-844-5100; appearing as counsel for defendant CAWC Waterproofing & Restoration.

Mr. Harley contests the court's tentative decision to overrule the demurrer to the cause of action for negligence. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court states that it is adopting its tentative ruling as to tenant harassment and negligent infliction of emotional distress, but is taking the matter under submission as to negligence. The court instructs Mr. Harley to prepare an order that conforms to the adopted portions of the tentative ruling, but with their proposed ruling as to their demurrer to the cause of action for negligence. The rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CAWC WATERPROOFING AND RESTORATION'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for negligence is taken under submission. Demurrer to the cause of action for tenant harassment is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to allege any facts for the proposition that the moving defendant for the "contractor of the landlord" within the meaning of SFRO and failed to suggest any potential amendment connecting the contractor to the landlord. Demurrer to the cause of action for Negligent Infliction of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend as neither the specific argument advanced in the demurrer, nor the authorities cited therein are mentioned in the opposition.

Counsel for defendant to submit a proposed order to the court for consideration. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
